Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Fixed nominal phrase with numeral taoʔk plus noun pok; used predicatively or as a discourse image.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: A small, concrete thing that represents a larger chance, task, or responsibility.
Usage Contexts: daily speech; advice; family planning; work decisions
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Built from common Pech imagery around counting and tangible objects; useful in speech about choosing carefully in household or village work.
First Example Sentence: taoʔk pok =i taaũh aye =ʔi tʃaberuri kaʔo as =yã.
Second Example Sentence: tas =ma taoʔk pok akari, ã =yã suwa taiʃkari.
Third Example Sentence: ĩ =kan taoʔk pok =ma ũtakaki, nĩhã ũtatũheri.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Reduplicated adverbial expression followed by verbal predicate; intensifies repeated movement or effort.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To keep trying again and again even when the path is difficult.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Echoic repetition resembles oral storytelling rhythm in Pech narratives and emphasizes persistence through repetition.
First Example Sentence: akãʃkwa ã taoʔk =mã kwi kwi kah aki, aryaha ũtakatʃaitVi =ras.
Second Example Sentence: tasuwa =yo kwi kwi kah aki, takaki =ma ausiʔ =ri.
Third Example Sentence: ĩ =ɲa kwi kwi kah aki, apuru piʃbarpi =ra.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Fixed verbal clause with lexicalized predicate; may stand alone as an evaluative comment.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Something has settled, finished, or come out clearly after confusion.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: the matter became clear or ended
Usage Contexts: daily conversation; clarification; family talk; storytelling
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Associated with ordinary narrative closure; useful when a speaker marks that an event or explanation has resolved.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Single lexicalized verbal form used as a compact idiomatic predicate.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To speak up, recount, or bring a remembered matter into the open.
Usage Contexts: storytelling; testimony; family memory; community meetings
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Frequently shaped by oral-history contexts where elders and speakers recount remembered events.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Pronominal/discourse particle sequence plus predicate; idiomatic clause used with stance marking.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To act as if the matter is already understood or obvious.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: say or do it as something obvious
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Draws on compact discourse particles common in Pech conversation; often carries speaker stance beyond literal content.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Adverbial element plus inflected verbal form; idiomatic predicate of completion or realization.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To finally understand, notice, or recognize what was hidden before.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Useful in narratives where realization follows repeated attempts or elders’ advice.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Possessive/topic phrase with evaluative lexical item; used as an idiomatic description.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: A person’s words carry weight, seriousness, or consequence.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Reflects the cultural value of careful public speech, counsel, and accountability in community contexts.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Nominal expression combining ethnonym/community term with locative or relational noun; idiomatic identity phrase.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To speak from Pech belonging, memory, and responsibility.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: act or speak as a Pech community member
Usage Contexts: cultural identity; education; public speech; heritage work
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Anchored in Pech self-reference and community continuity around Dulce Nombre de Culmí and surrounding Pech-speaking areas.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Deictic/discourse element plus verbal or nominal predicate; idiomatic time-and-place anchor.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Right there, at the decisive moment when a matter becomes visible.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Common narrative anchoring device, useful for locating decisive moments in spoken accounts.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Compact paired lexical phrase; idiomatic evaluative phrase used inside larger clauses.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: A matter feels uncertain, unstable, or hard to trust.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Uses brief paired words in an oral style that can compress doubt or social caution without a long explanation.
